objective: Move video generation execution, libTV runtime paths, generated files, exports, and long-running task work away from a local Windows desktop into a production API plus private worker model.
nonGoalsForFirstRelease: Direct mobile access to object storage credentials
Direct mobile access to libTV, SQLite, ffmpeg, or provider secrets

topology.publicClient: Mobile web, PWA, and packaged shells open https://www.zkraiflow.top only.
topology.apiService: Node API handles auth, validation, task creation, account data export, deletion request intake, and status reads.
topology.workerService: Private libTV worker handles long-running AI/video execution, ffmpeg, result import, and compliance evidence writing.
topology.storage: Mounted volume first, object storage adapter later. Public clients receive signed or proxied downloads only.
topology.database: Current SQLite can stay for pilot if mounted persistently; PostgreSQL is the target for public multi-user release.
topology.network: Worker is reachable only from the API service through a private network URL or a shared service token.

requiredEnvironment.name: RUN_STORAGE_DIR
requiredEnvironment.purpose: Persistent server-side directory for runs, uploads, exports, deletion requests, and generated results.

requiredEnvironment.name: TEXT_IMAGE_STORAGE_MODE
requiredEnvironment.purpose: Storage mode for text-to-image outputs and canvas media. Use mounted-volume for private pilot and object-storage before public multi-user release.

requiredEnvironment.name: LIBTV_BRIDGE_URL
requiredEnvironment.purpose: Private worker endpoint used by the API service. Must not point to localhost in production.

requiredEnvironment.name: LIBTV_WORKER_TOKEN
requiredEnvironment.purpose: Server-side service token used only by the API service when calling the private worker.

requiredEnvironment.name: LIBTV_REGISTER_SCRIPT
requiredEnvironment.purpose: Worker/container path or replacement service command for task registration.

requiredEnvironment.name: LIBTV_DB_PATH
requiredEnvironment.purpose: Mounted SQLite path or managed database reference visible to the worker.

requiredEnvironment.name: LIBTV_DEFAULT_DRY_RUN
requiredEnvironment.purpose: Launch guard. Keep true until worker, storage, and smoke tests pass; switch false only for controlled production execution.

requiredEnvironment.name: SMOKE_BASE_URL
requiredEnvironment.purpose: Public deployment target for post-release smoke tests.

workstreams.objective: Make uploads, generated runs, exports, and deletion request evidence survive process restarts and deployments.
ownerInputs: Choose mounted volume path or object storage bucket
Confirm retention policy for generated videos and exports
implementationSteps: Set RUN_STORAGE_DIR to a mounted production path.
Move upload, run, export, and deletion request writes under RUN_STORAGE_DIR.
Restart the API service and verify files remain readable.
Add object storage adapter only after pilot volume behavior is stable.
acceptanceCriteria: RUN_STORAGE_DIR is not empty and not a temporary path.
A generated run remains available after process restart.
Account export and deletion request evidence are written under the declared storage root.
cloud:check:strict no longer warns that RUN_STORAGE_DIR is missing.

workstreams.objective: Make text-to-image outputs and canvas node metadata durable, account-scoped, and ready for later object storage/PostgreSQL migration.
ownerInputs: Choose mounted volume or object storage bucket for generated images
Confirm retention policy for generated images and removed canvas nodes
Confirm PostgreSQL migration timing for text_image_canvas_nodes
implementationSteps: Keep local outputs/text-to-image only for desktop/private pilot.
Store generated images under persistent media storage before production pilot.
Move text_image_canvas_nodes to PostgreSQL before public multi-user release.
Store object_key, owner_user_id, tenant_id, file hash, MIME, size, prompt summary, model, and deleted_at.
Record textImageCanvasNodeId in AI evidence packs when images are reused in video workflows.
acceptanceCriteria: Generated text-image files remain readable after API restart.
Canvas nodes remain scoped by owner_user_id.
Removing a canvas node does not delete underlying image media unless retention cleanup explicitly runs.
npm run text-image:check continues to pass after storage changes.

workstreams.objective: Replace desktop-only libTV bridge, Windows paths, and local SQLite assumptions with a private worker service.
ownerInputs: Choose worker host: container platform, private VM, or managed job service
Confirm worker database path or managed database target
implementationSteps: Package libTV runner dependencies with Python, ffmpeg, and runtime credentials in the worker environment.
Expose private /healthz, /tasks, and /tasks/:id worker endpoints.
Point LIBTV_BRIDGE_URL to the private worker endpoint.
Replace Windows LIBTV_REGISTER_SCRIPT and LIBTV_DB_PATH values with worker/container paths.
Run dry-run generation first, then enable controlled real generation.

workerSmokeEvidenceTemplate.purpose: Record sanitized proof for worker health, private auth rejection, dry-run submit, idempotency, polling, cancel, failure mapping, result import, and restart/resume before real video generation is opened.

acceptanceCriteria: Worker health check reports storageWritable, libtvReady, and ffmpegReady.
API can submit a dry-run task to the worker and poll status.
API can import worker result references into the existing task/result view.
cloud:check:strict no longer reports localhost or Windows libTV paths.

workstreams.objective: Prevent public clients from calling worker endpoints directly.
ownerInputs: Choose private network routing or service token
implementationSteps: Keep worker endpoints off the public internet when possible.
If public routing is unavoidable, require a server-side service token.
Never expose worker token, provider keys, database path, or storage credentials to browser code.
acceptanceCriteria: Mobile web and packaged app can call only the public API service.
Worker endpoints reject unauthenticated browser requests.
No worker secret appears in frontend source, manifest, or public pages.

workstreams.objective: Keep long-running video jobs observable, retryable, and limited during pilot release.
ownerInputs: Confirm failure categories shown to users
implementationSteps: Use idempotent task IDs for submit and retry.
Record task events for queued, running, failed, cancelled, and succeeded states.
Limit worker concurrency before real generation is enabled.
Surface user-readable failure reasons in the mobile notification panel and task cards.
acceptanceCriteria: Duplicate task submit does not create duplicate paid generation.
Failed worker task shows a clear user-facing reason.
Retry and cancellation behavior is documented before public launch.

workstreams.objective: Verify deployment, worker, storage, and rollback before external users rely on the app.
implementationSteps: Run cloud:smoke against https://www.zkraiflow.top after deploy.
Create one dry-run video task and confirm status polling.
Restart API and worker services, then confirm generated artifacts still exist.
Record the previous release artifact and environment snapshot for rollback.
acceptanceCriteria: cloud:smoke passes against public HTTPS.
Storage restart test passes.
Worker health check passes after restart.
Rollback procedure is documented and assigned.

requiredEvidence: Public pages and PWA assets pass smoke checks
Owner understands libTV remains gated until worker is verified
Worker health, dry-run, and one controlled real generation smoke pass
Contact, privacy, retention, and review account values are final
Native Android/iOS builds are verified on device
Store screenshots are captured from final native builds
Privacy/Data safety/AI disclosure materials are final
